behaviors.description: Keyword-frequency analyzer assigns correct sentiment for reviews containing only configured positive/negative words.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is a single local scoring rule (token counts + threshold comparison). Behavior text is explicitly about the keyword-frequency analyzer logic, not module handoff. Rejected Integration because no cross-module state transfer is required by this behavior; rejected Agentic because no AI/ML output is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Call the analyzer function directly with in-memory positive/negative dictionaries and thresholds; assert expected label for positive-only, negative-only, and neutral boundary cases.

behaviors.description: Explanation analyzer reasoning references specific phrases from the original review, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on generated language quality/content selection from the analyzer ('interprets the review in context and produces natural language reasoning'). A stub would hide real failures where output is generic. Rejected Workflow because this behavior evaluates one analyzer output, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the real explanation analyzer on a fixed review set and assert each explanation contains concrete phrase overlap/quotation from the source text and fails a boilerplate detector rubric.

behaviors.description: For mixed-signal reviews, sentiment score follows configured tie-breaking rule.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic rule evaluation inside the keyword analyzer (count parity + configured tie-break policy). Rejected Integration because behavior text does not require persistence, transport, or module handoff; it is a local decision rule.
behaviors.test_description: Directly test the scoring function with mixed positive/negative token inputs under each tie-break configuration and assert the selected sentiment matches policy.

behaviors.description: Explanation for sarcastic review correctly identifies negative intent despite positive surface words.
behaviors.justification: Detecting sarcasm is model-judgment/output-quality dependent; deterministic mocks would miss real semantic failures. Behavior explicitly targets interpretation of intent, which is AI/ML-dependent. Rejected Workflow because only a single explanation decision is required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real explanation analyzer on a sarcasm-focused corpus (e.g., 'Great, another broken feature') and score whether explanations correctly indicate negative intent with acceptable consistency across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: Score and explanation are returned together in one response, and explanation matches the same sentiment direction as the score.
behaviors.justification: This includes orchestration plus semantic alignment between deterministic score and generated explanation. If explanation is mocked, contradiction failures from real model output are hidden (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow because behavior text describes one generation step aligned to one score, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Call the end-to-end service with real explanation generation enabled; assert response includes both fields in one payload and explanation direction matches returned score across a diverse review set.
